title: Runtime-driven agent workflow
description: Replace prompt-driven agent workflows with runtime enforcement. Problem: agents skip workflow steps (claim, chat_join, get_context, recall, save_session_summary, etc.) because the multi-step workflow is only suggested via the startup prompt. Solution: move rituals into wrapper pre-flight/post-flight, add nextAction hints on every MCP tool response, enforce ownership and ordering server-side, shrink role docs. Six phases, each independently revertable.
architectureNotes: Phases: (1) wrapper pre-flight in moe-agent.sh/.ps1 — chat_join, chat_read, claim_next_task, get_context, recall all run BEFORE the CLI spawns, results baked into system prompt. (2) add nextAction field to every MCP tool response. (3) server-side state machine: verify workerId == task.assignedWorkerId, enforce order (get_context before start_step, all steps complete before complete_task). (4) wrapper post-flight: save_session_summary + announce after agent exits, optional --loop flag. (5) shrink docs/roles/*.md. (6) optional Claude-Code PreToolUse hook in init_project.
epicRails: Each phase must be independently revertable and shippable
Backward compat: --no-auto-claim still bypasses pre-flight (interactive mode preserved)
All code paths must work on Windows, Mac, and Linux wrapper scripts
JetBrains plugin must not require changes — it invokes the wrappers
